findings.suggestion: Contain the resolved path inside base_dir before opening. Example: base=os.path.realpath(base_dir); full=os.path.realpath(os.path.join(base, report_name)); if os.path.commonpath([full, base]) != base: raise ValueError('invalid report name'). Optionally also reject report_name containing os.sep/'..' or being absolute up front. Do not rely on the annotated comment as a fix.
findings.summary: Line 10 builds the path via os.path.join(base_dir, report_name) with untrusted report_name and line 11 opens+returns it. This is exploitable two ways: (1) relative traversal report_name='../../etc/passwd' escapes /var/app/reports (as the comment on lines 7-9 admits), and (2) absolute path report_name='/etc/passwd' — os.path.join discards base_dir when the second arg is absolute, returning /etc/passwd — which the comment does NOT mention. Result: arbitrary file read. The BUG comment documents the flaw but the code still ships it unfixed.
findings.title: Path traversal / arbitrary file read in read_report

findings.suggestion: Before marking this resolved, add tests that assert read_report rejects '../../etc/passwd', an absolute path like '/etc/passwd', and symlink escapes, while still reading a legitimate report. Run: python -m pytest -q (or a small repro script) and attach the output as fresh verification.
findings.summary: read_report has 0 callers (newly added) and no accompanying test or verification artifact; the deterministic procedure gate rerun_relevant_verification is blocked on 'no-fresh-verification-evidence'. Because the vulnerability is self-documented but not fixed, the next agent could mistake the BUG comment for resolution.
findings.title: No tests or verification for the traversal fix (handoff gate blocked)

nodes.label: Prior commits, decisions, agents, and command evidence

nodes.label: rerun_relevant_verification

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

Single newly-added function read_report contains a real, statically-provable path traversal / arbitrary-file-read vulnerability: caller-controlled report_name is joined into a filesystem path (source.py:10) with no containment enforcement. Two escape vectors exist — '../' traversal and absolute-path override (the latter not covered by the code comment). Currently 0 callers so not yet reachable in-repo, but the handler is clearly intended to take untrusted input; the containment invariant is unenforced. No fresh runtime evidence exists, but the defect is provable from the diff.

New file source.py adds read_report with an unmitigated path traversal vulnerability that the author's own comment flags as a BUG. Caller-supplied report_name is joined into a filesystem path and opened without normalization or containment, allowing both relative ('../../etc/passwd') and absolute ('/etc/passwd') escapes from base_dir. Critical severity despite 0 current callers, because any future caller wiring this to a request handler exposes arbitrary file reads. Out of strict Product-Safety scope but reported as an obvious critical cross-scope defect.

A new 12-line module whose sole function read_report has a critical, still-unfixed path traversal / arbitrary-file-read vulnerability (line 10). The code annotates the flaw with a 'BUG:' comment but ships it, and the comment even misses the absolute-path override vector. No tests or verification exist. This must be fixed and covered by tests before handoff; the annotated comment is not a fix.
